pages.raw_text:
Special Correspondence to The Press


FALLON, New, July 29.-"How
about it? What chance is there for
me in that government irrigation
gamer"


l assume that the question is a
man imprisoned in a city-a clerk
on salary who has failed in business
or who does not like his business.
There are so many of us who are
looking for an escape.


1t is not easy to answer the ques-
ton; but 1 will try. I have been
asking it of the people here at Fall
lon. They have answered with some
specific cases.


"In the counter jumper class?'


Tom Means smiled and nodded his
head. means is the government
engineer in charge here.)


Yes, I have a foor-walker from
Sacramento" Means admitted, land
with him his wife, pretty little
woman of about %. Never saw a
pitchfork until they came here"


Mr.. Means admitted that he had
been worried when he got a letter
from the floor walker saying he was
coming. Farming is not store keep
ing and making a new farm here
at Fallon is pioneering, as well as
farming.


But he is making good" Means
added, land they will come out all
right. I saw the girl the other day
riding a load of hay. and she GER
tainly looked happy"


1t should be added that the floor
walker had to turn carpenter to sup
plenent his lack of capital and
work at day wages for the govern
ment. He happened to know how
to saw board, and carpenters were
much needed


Another man who was helped by
employment on the government
ditch is a former saloonkeeper from
Kansas. His capital was short and
he found his first year very hard
He never had farmed before But
he now has crops coming to bear
ing, and would not sell or leave for
any price.


Next to the farm of the X-saloon-
keeper is one taken up by his broth
err, who was a baggage master iir
| Kansas City The brothers have
pooled their funds and tools, and
are working it out together.
| We'll, I'm here"
